cells.p: TAM Reconciliation Bridge - Dual-Narrative (plan Section 2.3, formula-driven)

Top-down (Narrative B, OP-5 avail-category + MSC + SCN + USCG) vs bottom-up (Narrative A, FPDS 65 MRO PSCs). Every $ cell is a SUMIFS or named-range formula - click to see source. Grand totals must reconcile to ~$16-17B TD vs ~$9.0B BU; the ~$7-8B gap is dominated by Public NSY intramural labor ($7.5B structural, Row 2).
OP-5 Table IV 11 private avail cats

SUMIFS(Awards[FY2025 Obligation],Awards[Is MRO],"Y",Awards[Canonical Office],"SWRMC")/1000000+SUMIFS(Awards[FY2025 Obligation],Awards[Is MRO],"Y",Awards[Canonical Office],"MARMC")/1000000+SUMIFS(Awards[FY2025 Obligation],Awards[Is MRO],"Y",Awards[Canonical Office],"SERMC")/1000000+SUMIFS(Awards[FY2025 Obligation],Awards[Is MRO],"Y",Awards[Canonical Office],"NW RMC / Puget Sound")/1000000+SUMIFS(Awards[FY2025 Obligation],Awards[Is MRO],"Y",Awards[Canonical Office],"Pearl Harbor RMC")/1000000+SUMIFS(Awards[FY2025 Obligation],Awards[Is MRO],"Y",Awards[Canonical Office],"SRF-JRMC Yokosuka")/1000000+SUMIFS(Awards[FY2025 Obligation],Awards[Is MRO],"Y",Awards[Canonical Office],"FDRMC Naples")/1000000
Primary reconciliation test (plan §6.5). Gap should be within +/-10%. Drivers: FY25 Current vs FPDS actual timing; FDNF scope boundary; trade PSCs not at RMC buyers.
OP-5 Table IV 4 public NSY rows
STRUCTURAL GAP. FPDS captures contract awards only; public NSY labor is intramural federal workforce, invisible by construction. NOT addressable by BuildCo.
FY25 MSC M&R executes out of 1B1B; transfers to 1B4B in FY26. Should reconcile within +/-15%. Note: MSC category split not source-explicit for FY25.
FDNF private MSRA (Yokosuka / Rota / Bahrain / Sigonella)
MEMO ROW - not double-counted. FDNF MSRA is captured in the OP-5 private subtotal and in the FPDS SRF-JRMC / FDRMC canonical buyers within Row 1. Split-out here for narrative clarity only.
SCN CVN RCOH (LI 2086) + embedded complex OH
Multi-year incremental funding vs single-FY FPDS obligation - mismatch expected. PSC1905 MRO-bucket includes more than just CVN RCOH (nuclear overhauls, SSN inactivations).
Captured within FPDS 65 PSCs; not separable from OMN-funded

SOURCED. Distinct OPN appropriation (1810N) established by PL 116-93 (Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2020) for private contracted CONUS surface + submarine maintenance at CPF / FFC. Same avail vocabulary as OMN 1B4B (OH, SRA, PIA, P-MA, RA/TA, CIA, DPIA, DSRA, SCO -- defined OPN_BA1_Book lines 21217-21268). FY25 enacted $2,392.190M -- replaces prior $500M plug (4.8x underweight). No double-count vs OMN 1B4B: 1810N OPN is parallel funding stream to 1804N OMN.
(plan estimate ~$500M — no clean WPN ship-MRO line item)
Captured in J010/J012/J014/N010 etc.; not isolable

PLAN ESTIMATE - genuinely not source-pinnable. WPN_Book P-40 exhibits structure WPN as missile/torpedo procurement (BA01 Ballistic Missiles $1.80B, BA02 Other Missiles $3.40B, BA03 Torpedoes $0.74B, BA04 Other Weapons $0.17B, BA06 Spares $0.24B FY25). Mods lines (Trident II Mods $1.79B, Tomahawk Mods $0.30B, etc.) are weapons-system-level sustainment, not ship depot work. Shipboard installation flows under separate (typically OMN) contracts and is not isolable from WPN P-40s. The $500M plug is a judgment estimate of WPN $ that flows to FPDS combat-systems-related MRO PSCs.
Services sheet USCG TAM named range

CONFIDENCE = LOW on top-down. USCG has no OP-5 Table IV equivalent. ISVS $120M is PC&I floor; actual USCG cutter MRO believed $300-500M but not source-pinned. Bottom-up FPDS is the better anchor here (flip vs Navy pattern).
Top-down minus bottom-up = total gap. Dominant component: Public NSY $7.5B (Row 2) structural.
Starting point for top-down private-addressable derivation. Further subtract captive SUPSHIP complex OH + FMS on Private Addressable sheet.
